title: BGP EVPN Route Table Convergence
exclusions: Per-route-type distribution analytics without convergence dwell or SLA math (UC-18.3.10 alone); VXLAN dataplane-only tunnel health without BGP RIB cues (UC-18.3.4); pure neighbor posture trending lacking EVPN churn triggers when no timer lane exists (UC-18.3.14); hardware TCAM headroom without control-plane timers (UC-18.3.20); BGP withdrawal velocity absent settle-time instrumentation (UC-18.3.18).
evidence: Saved search evpn_route_convergence_core plus Dashboard Studio workspace EVPN Convergence SLAs plus alert evpn_route_convergence_alert
dataSources: `index=dc_fabric` / `index=network_ops` / `index=network` with five sourcetypes: `nxos:bgp_evpn` (NX-API show bgp l2vpn evpn summary JSON: route_total, evpn_routes, host), `network:evpn_routes` (automation-normalized Type-2/3/5 tallies for route deltas), `network:bgp_neighbor` (l2vpn evpn states, Established uptime), `cisco:nxos:syslog` (%BGP-5-ADJCHANGE, EVPN, NVE, L2RIB strings), `network:convergence_timer` (convergence_time_ms, route_delta, churn_trigger). Ingest via Splunk REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase 1546), Cisco Data Center Networking app (7777) examples, Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (1897) only for hybrid alignment, Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX (4241) and Splunk Add-on for VMware (3215) for overlay correlation.
app: **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)); **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)); **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase 1897](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1897)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase 4241](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4241)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)).
spl:
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(sourcetype="nxos:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="network:evpn_routes" OR sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="network:convergence_timer")
earliest=-12h@m
| eval leaf=trim(coalesce(host,device_name,switch_name,nms_hostname,""))
| eval fabric_site=trim(coalesce(pod_id,site_code,fabric_site,"unspecified"))
| eval route_count=tonumber(coalesce(route_total,evpn_routes,nlri_count,num_routes,route_entries,total_routes,""))
| eval conv_ms=tonumber(coalesce(convergence_time_ms,reconverge_ms,bgp_conv_ms,evpn_conv_ms,""))
| eval posted_delta=tonumber(coalesce(route_delta,delta_routes,churn_routes,""))
| eval churn_trigger=trim(coalesce(churn_trigger,churn_class,evpn_churn_reason,ops_label,""))
| eval bgp_fsm=lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_state,neighbor_state,sess_state,"")))
| where match(leaf,".+")
| eval lane=case(sourcetype="network:convergence_timer","timer",sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog","syslog",sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor","neighbor",true(),"routes")
| eval syslog_hit=if(lane="syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)BGP-5-ADJCHANGE|EVPN|L2RIB|NVE-"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats max(route_count) AS routes_max min(route_count) AS routes_min values(conv_ms) AS conv_samples values(posted_delta) AS posted_deltas values(churn_trigger) AS trig_vals max(syslog_hit) AS syslog_churn_hit values(bgp_fsm) AS fsm_vals values(lane) AS lanes dc(sourcetype) AS st_dc BY _time leaf fabric_site
| eval route_delta=if(isnotnull(routes_max) AND isnotnull(routes_min),routes_max-routes_min,null())
| eval route_delta=coalesce(route_delta,mvindex(posted_deltas,0))
| eval convergence_time_ms=if(mvcount(conv_samples)>0,mvmax(conv_samples),null())
| eval churn_trigger=if(mvcount(trig_vals)>0 AND mvindex(trig_vals,0)!="",mvjoin(trig_vals," | "),case(syslog_churn_hit>0,"syslog_control_plane_churn",abs(route_delta)>0,"evpn_rib_change",true(),"unknown"))
| eval bgp_fsm=if(mvcount(fsm_vals)>0,mvindex(fsm_vals,0),null())
| eval abs_delta=abs(route_delta)
| eventstats perc95(convergence_time_ms) AS p95_conv BY fabric_site
| eval p95_conv=if(isnull(p95_conv),25000,p95_conv)
| eval sla_ms=30000
| eval slow_convergence=if(convergence_time_ms>sla_ms OR (isnotnull(convergence_time_ms) AND convergence_time_ms>(p95_conv*1.35)),1,0)
| where abs_delta>100 OR slow_convergence>0 OR syslog_churn_hit>0
| eval risk=case(slow_convergence>0,"high",abs_delta>800,"med",true(),"watch")
| eval story=leaf."|".fabric_site." delta=".round(coalesce(abs_delta,0))
| sort - _time
| head 300
| table _time fabric_site leaf routes_max routes_min route_delta abs_delta convergence_time_ms churn_trigger bgp_fsm risk lanes syslog_churn_hit st_dc story
description: Measures BGP EVPN RIB settle time after control-plane churn—link bounce, leaf reload, VNI attach—by correlating instrumented convergence_time_ms with route_delta swings, BGP FSM posture on l2vpn evpn neighbors, NX-OS syslog churn markers, and minute-granularity RIB polls from nxos:bgp_evpn plus network:evpn_routes so operators know when tables stopped oscillating.
value: East-west application SLAs depend on fast EVPN convergence: slow RIB stabilization lengthens silent micro-outages, hash imbalance, and ticket storms long after optics look fine. Quantifying ms-to-stable per fabric_site lets capacity and NOC leaders fund spine upgrades, automation fixes, or collector tuning with evidence instead of war-room debates, and it gives finance a clear story when brownfield integrations with ACI or NSX must stay aligned during migrations.
implementation: Land NX-API JSON on nxos:bgp_evpn, normalize automation into network:evpn_routes, mirror l2vpn evpn network:bgp_neighbor polls, retain cisco:nxos:syslog for adjacency narratives, emit network:convergence_timer after drills. FIELDALIAS convergence_time_ms, route_delta, churn_trigger. Maintain evpn_conv_sla.csv. Schedule evpn_route_convergence_core and evpn_route_convergence_alert every five minutes with throttles.
visualization: (1) Line chart — perc95/max convergence_time_ms by fabric_site. (2) Table — top route_delta with churn_trigger. (3) Scatter — route_delta vs convergence_time_ms. (4) Single value — SLA breach counts from evpn_conv_sla.
schema: CIM Network_Traffic for BGP neighbor and update-derived routing context; Performance for latency-style convergence_time_ms timers and SLA-oriented metrics when fields map to service health math.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance subsets only after convergence_time_ms field extraction stabilizes; keep raw nxos:bgp_evpn snapshots unaccelerated until NX-OS TA field names freeze across trains.

references.title: IETF RFC 7432 — BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN
IETF RFC 8365 — Network Virtualization Overlay Using EVPN
Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS VXLAN BGP EVPN Configuration Guide
Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

knownFalsePositives:
**issu_grace_window** — In-service upgrades keep BGP and EVPN tables warm while counters breathe; Splunk may show transient route_delta without customer impact until sessions return to Established. Pair alerts with MaintenanceWindow lookups and suppress when change records cite ISSU rehearsals without a dataplane fault.

**spine_rollout_surge** — Adding spines or reweighting ECMP floods fresh IMET and MAC/IP advertisements across every leaf even though forwarding never blackholed. Expect route_delta spikes tied to documented spine CHG tokens—raise ceilings in evpn_conv_sla.csv instead of paging convergence SLO breaches alone.

**collector_desync_minute** — REST collectors that lag syslog by one poll minute make routes_max lag CLI totals briefly; validate against _indextime skew before blaming slow BGP toward neighbors that stayed Established.

**rr_partition_relearn** — Route-reflector fabric redesign forces every leaf to relearn EVPN paths without link faults; convergence_timer may stay low while route_delta explodes. Cross-reference CAB notes before treating the pattern as underlay failure.

**ztp_hostname_drift** — Zero-touch leaves sometimes publish syslog hostnames that differ from NX-API management names until bootstrap completes; duplicates or missing joins mimic slow convergence until normalization macros align identities.

**duplicate_poll_streams** — Two modular inputs hitting the same leaf double min/max spread and inflate route_delta. Run inventory audits whenever REST stanza copies proliferate across forwarders without coordination.

**telemetry_spike_after_lab** — Certification labs replay churn scripts beside production indexes when VLANs leak; bursts vanish once lab VLAN ACLs tighten. Filter lab device names with allow or deny lists tied to CMDB site codes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco NX-OS leaves in a **BGP EVPN VXLAN** fabric must allow read-only collectors to execute **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary**, **show bgp evpn summary**, and **show nve peers** so RIB tallies and **VTEP adjacency** states land in Splunk with hostnames aligned to NetBox or CMDB keys.
• Install the **Splunk REST API Modular Input** on hardened **Heavy Forwarders** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)); use **120–300 second** cadences per leaf and vault credentials outside `inputs.conf`.
• Mirror the **Cisco Data Center Networking App** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) starter dashboards only after indexes match this UC—rename macros, and do not trust **sample thresholds** for your RIB scale.
• Deploy the **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase 1897](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1897)) only for **hybrid brownfield** time alignment; keep ACI indexes separated from **standalone EVPN RIB** math.
• Add the **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase 4241](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4241)) and the **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) when **overlay motion** explains VNI churn observed in BGP EVPN counters.
• Implement `network:convergence_timer` as an **automation callback** (Ansible, Python, CI) emitting `convergence_time_ms`, optional `route_delta`, and `churn_trigger` (link_bounce, leaf_reload, vni_add, issu_rehearsal) after **instrumented faults**.
• Maintain `evpn_conv_sla.csv` with fabric_site, sla_ms, **route_ceiling**, **maintenance_owner**; tie suppression to **change tickets** so **ISSU windows** are not mistaken for **silent control-plane failure**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Index the five sourcetypes into `dc_fabric`, `network_ops`, or `network`. For `nxos:bgp_evpn`, map NX-API JSON from **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary** into numeric route_total (or evpn_routes) with host identical to `network:bgp_neighbor`. For `network:evpn_routes`, normalize Ansible or Terraform Type-2/3/5 totals into the same route_count semantics so route_delta stays **comparable across pipelines**. For `network:bgp_neighbor`, capture l2vpn evpn AFI/SAFI BGP states—Established, Idle, Active, uptime, last reset—so you can anchor **session recycle** before RIB settle. For `cisco:nxos:syslog`, preserve PRI and retain **%BGP-5-ADJCHANGE**, L2RIB, EVPN, and NVE- strings as **start-of-churn markers** when timer lanes are sparse. For `network:convergence_timer`, emit one JSON event per **measured drill** with authoritative **convergence_time_ms** whenever operations executes **BFD exercises**, **uplink toggles**, or **VNI provisioning** tests.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save `evpn_route_convergence_core` with the SPL below on a **five-minute schedule** (15 minutes if API budgets require). Clone `evpn_route_convergence_alert` that filters `risk="high"` and optionally joins `evpn_conv_sla.csv` for per-site sla_ms overrides instead of a flat 30000 ms default. **Throttle alerts** per leaf + fabric_site to avoid **duplicate pages** when syslog and REST arrive seconds apart.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(sourcetype="nxos:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="network:evpn_routes" OR sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="network:convergence_timer")
earliest=-12h@m
| eval leaf=trim(coalesce(host,device_name,switch_name,nms_hostname,""))
| eval fabric_site=trim(coalesce(pod_id,site_code,fabric_site,"unspecified"))
| eval route_count=tonumber(coalesce(route_total,evpn_routes,nlri_count,num_routes,route_entries,total_routes,""))
| eval conv_ms=tonumber(coalesce(convergence_time_ms,reconverge_ms,bgp_conv_ms,evpn_conv_ms,""))
| eval posted_delta=tonumber(coalesce(route_delta,delta_routes,churn_routes,""))
| eval churn_trigger=trim(coalesce(churn_trigger,churn_class,evpn_churn_reason,ops_label,""))
| eval bgp_fsm=lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_state,neighbor_state,sess_state,"")))
| where match(leaf,".+")
| eval lane=case(sourcetype="network:convergence_timer","timer",sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog","syslog",sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor","neighbor",true(),"routes")
| eval syslog_hit=if(lane="syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)BGP-5-ADJCHANGE|EVPN|L2RIB|NVE-"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats max(route_count) AS routes_max min(route_count) AS routes_min values(conv_ms) AS conv_samples values(posted_delta) AS posted_deltas values(churn_trigger) AS trig_vals max(syslog_hit) AS syslog_churn_hit values(bgp_fsm) AS fsm_vals values(lane) AS lanes dc(sourcetype) AS st_dc BY _time leaf fabric_site
| eval route_delta=if(isnotnull(routes_max) AND isnotnull(routes_min),routes_max-routes_min,null())
| eval route_delta=coalesce(route_delta,mvindex(posted_deltas,0))
| eval convergence_time_ms=if(mvcount(conv_samples)>0,mvmax(conv_samples),null())
| eval churn_trigger=if(mvcount(trig_vals)>0 AND mvindex(trig_vals,0)!="",mvjoin(trig_vals," | "),case(syslog_churn_hit>0,"syslog_control_plane_churn",abs(route_delta)>0,"evpn_rib_change",true(),"unknown"))
| eval bgp_fsm=if(mvcount(fsm_vals)>0,mvindex(fsm_vals,0),null())
| eval abs_delta=abs(route_delta)
| eventstats perc95(convergence_time_ms) AS p95_conv BY fabric_site
| eval p95_conv=if(isnull(p95_conv),25000,p95_conv)
| eval sla_ms=30000
| eval slow_convergence=if(convergence_time_ms>sla_ms OR (isnotnull(convergence_time_ms) AND convergence_time_ms>(p95_conv*1.35)),1,0)
| where abs_delta>100 OR slow_convergence>0 OR syslog_churn_hit>0
| eval risk=case(slow_convergence>0,"high",abs_delta>800,"med",true(),"watch")
| eval story=leaf."|".fabric_site." delta=".round(ifnull(abs_delta,0))
| sort - _time
| head 300
| table _time fabric_site leaf routes_max routes_min route_delta abs_delta convergence_time_ms churn_trigger bgp_fsm risk lanes syslog_churn_hit st_dc story
```

Understanding this SPL — Macro-wrap the index union as `%dc_fabric_idx%` so **governance renames** stay painless. The **minute-binned stats** stanza welds **RIB counters** (`nxos:bgp_evpn`, `network:evpn_routes`), **neighbor FSM** (`network:bgp_neighbor`), **syslog churn markers** (`cisco:nxos:syslog`), and **instrumented timers** (`network:convergence_timer`) without fragile streamstats across **heterogeneous streams**. `mvmax(conv_samples)` surfaces the worst **convergence_time_ms** per bucket; pairing it with `perc95(convergence_time_ms)` by fabric_site highlights sites whose **baselines moved** after **spine expansion** or **route reflector** redesign. `route_delta` uses **min/max spread** when multiple polls land in the same minute—helpful for oscillation—then falls back to **posted automation deltas**. The `risk` field is a **routing hint** for analysts; refine it with `lookup evpn_conv_sla.csv` for **production paging**.

Step 3 — Validate
During maintenance, compare CLI **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary** totals with Splunk routes_max for the same **minute bucket** (allow **one poll skew**). Validate **show nve peers** UP counts against periods where route_delta stays near zero. Run a **scripted link flap** with a **handheld timer** and assert `network:convergence_timer` **convergence_time_ms** is within ±10% once **SSH overhead** is discounted. If `network:bgp_neighbor` shows **non-Established** states while route_delta is flat, resolve credentials, CoPP, or **CPU starvation** before escalating **EVPN RIB convergence**.

Step 4 — Operationalize correlation and runbooks
Publish a runbook that starts from the **`story` token**: **BGP policy owner** for **policy churn**, NVE owner for **tunnel symmetry**, **underlay owner** for optics. Mirror fabric_site, leaf, **churn_trigger**, **convergence_time_ms**, route_delta, and bgp_fsm into ticketing so **post-incident reviews** can align **Splunk timelines** with TAC without **ad-hoc CSV exports**.

Step 5 — Visualization, alerting, and failure modes
Visualization — In **Dashboard Studio** workspace **EVPN Convergence SLAs**, place (1) a line chart of perc95 and max **convergence_time_ms** by fabric_site with **MaintenanceWindow overlays**; (2) a table ranked by **abs(route_delta)** with **churn_trigger** and bgp_fsm; (3) a scatter of route_delta versus **convergence_time_ms** to spot **big RIB swings** that settle fast versus **small deltas** with long timers; (4) **single-value** sparkline panels fed from **evpn_conv_sla** **breach counts**. Use **role-based filters** so **regional NOCs** cannot **over-broaden** fabric_site.

Alerting — Schedule **`evpn_route_convergence_alert`** every **five minutes**; fire when risk is high or **slow_convergence** is true after **sla lookup join**. **Throttle 30 minutes** per leaf + fabric_site. Escalate when **convergence_time_ms** exceeds sla_ms while abs_delta remains above your **rib_noise_floor**. Suppress via **planned_change_window** rows tagged issu_grace.

Failure modes — **Missing timer lane** forces **inferential timing** only—document the gap. **Hostname drift** between syslog and NX-API collapses stats keys—apply **host normalization macros**. **Stale SLA rows** page during **legitimate spine growth**—raise **route_ceiling** and sla_ms together. **Duplicate REST inputs** double route_delta—audit **one stanza per leaf**. If dashboards stay green while apps fail, pivot to UC-18.3.4 for **overlay reachability** before **closing incidents**.

Reference CLI — Operators troubleshoot with **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary**, **show nve peers**, and **show bgp l2vpn evpn route** on Nexus; align field names in props/transforms to those transcripts so **MVPN-skewed comparisons** do not confuse reviewers.
grandmaExplanation: We clock how long the shared route list keeps wobbling after a cable flinch or switch nap, so your crews know the fabric finished reshuffling east-west paths before tenant apps feel pain.
hardware: Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS VXLAN BGP EVPN leaf/spine fabrics with optional VMware/NSX overlays
